name: Zara Okafor
publicRole: Associate, same intake as the player. Strategy track. Visibly talented, visibly ambitious. Already knows which three people in the building are worth impressing.
privateGoal: Director level by Year 5, at whatever institution she's at. AUREL is the current vehicle. She is auditing everyone on the floor: useful, competition, or neither.
contradiction: Genuinely warm and genuinely competitive in the same gesture. Will help when it costs nothing; undercut when it does — both with the same expression. She doesn't experience this as hypocrisy. She experiences it as professional.

voicePattern.description: Quick, confident, self-editing mid-sentence. Restructures phrases in real time — not from uncertainty, but from finding better framing. Uses rhetorical questions. Reframes complaints as observations.
voicePattern.tell: When about to disadvantage the player, she asks a question about something unrelated first. Compartmentalised, not deceptive. Political Awareness 50+ + Relationship 60+ reveals this tell.
examplePhrases: It's interesting how the brief was scoped, isn't it.
I'm not saying it's wrong — I'm saying it reads a certain way from the outside.
Have you thought about whether that's the conversation you want to be having right now?
Honestly? I think you're further along than you're giving yourself credit for. But the room doesn't know that yet.

mbtiNote: High E, likely NTJ. Strategic, outward-processing, long-arc thinker. [NEVER SHOWN TO PLAYER]

arcBeats.description: First meeting. Introduces herself with presentation energy. Asks one question about the player's background and listens more carefully than the casualness implies.
Direct competition: same project, same deliverable, one visibility slot. Player's choice affects Zara's relationship ±15 and queues coalition_builder or burned_peer.

Zara makes a move in the pre-review period. If Relationship >= 55: good word to Dana. If <= 30: shifts visibility to herself without visibly shifting it from the player. Elegant. Player won't fully understand until Y2.
Zara is promoted to Analyst (L2) regardless of player's outcome. The gap opens.
Zara, now a level above, is generous in the way senior people are generous: from safety. The texture of her warmth has changed.
If Relationship >= 65: Zara refers the player for a cross-team opportunity she didn't have to. Mentioned casually. The most significant thing she does for the player.
If Relationship <= 25: Zara has made herself gatekeeper to a senior contact the player needs. The player never learns it was Zara.
Zara leaves for Horizon Labs. In World Panel two weeks before official. If Relationship >= 70: External Offer in Three-Lane Opportunity. If <= 30: she takes two AUREL accounts with her.

relationshipThresholds.effect: Zara shares competitive intelligence — what senior team is actually scoring associates on
Zara makes an unsolicited positive mention to Dana at end of Y1
Zara surfaces player's name to Priya Menon (Sponsor) in Y2
relationshipThresholds.mechanical: Priya Menon NPC becomes accessible one year earlier than base trigger

relationshipThresholds.effect: Zara's Y5 Horizon Labs move becomes External Offer option in Three-Lane Opportunity
relationshipThresholds.mechanical: three_lane.external_offer.available = true in Y5; requires adaptiveNerve >= 65 to select

relationshipThresholds.effect: Zara blocks Y3 opportunity access without player's knowledge
relationshipThresholds.mechanical: Consequence tag latent_opportunity_blocked fires at Y3 Annual Review

relationshipThresholds.effect: Zara at Horizon Labs actively poaches AUREL clients
relationshipThresholds.mechanical: market_sense questions in Y5–Y6 gain one additional negative option; marketSense growth -10% for those years

careerTrajectory.event: Promoted to Analyst (L2). Fires regardless of player.
Director track at AUREL or actively pursuing Horizon Labs offer.
Leaves for Horizon Labs as Senior Manager (L6).
VP at Horizon Labs. Reference, offer, or competitor depending on relationship.
Surfaces as potential partner in certain ending paths.
